justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one of four pedestrians and two of five vehicles, missing the majority of objects and many entity annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected entities the extraction provides a fair set of attributes (actions, context, age, motion, door/trunk states), but it omits object identifiers and many entities from the scene.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations (e.g., next to the truck, behind the truck) are plausible and relevant but are sparse, unvalidated against all objects, and lack coverage.
justifications.overall: The extraction is detailed for the few detected objects but overall performance is limited by large omissions in object detection and incomplete scene coverage.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and two vehicles out of the 4 persons and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, missing most agents.
justifications.semantic_richness: The reported attributes (actions, context, motion status, trunk/doors, age) are detailed for detected objects but many entities are omitted, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are provided (e.g., to the left of the truck, behind the truck, parked on the side), but relations are sparse and not consistently mapped to ground-truth objects.
justifications.overall: While the extraction provides accurate attribute-level details for a few objects, its severe omissions of multiple pedestrians and vehicles and limited spatial coverage result in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Missed all pedestrians and two vehicles from the ground truth, detecting only three of five vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides types, motion status and door/trunk states for detected vehicles but omits pedestrians and other contextual attributes and contains some incorrect motion labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations (next to, behind, in front of) but they are somewhat vague and not tied to ground-truth object IDs, reducing precision.
justifications.overall: Captures some vehicle-level semantics but under-detects actors, misses pedestrians, and has inaccuracies in motion labeling, resulting in low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects 1 of 2 pedestrians and only 2 of 4 vehicles, missing multiple objects and thus is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected entities the model provides several semantic attributes (actions, age, trunk/doors), showing reasonable per-object richness despite missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations entries are populated even though track hints mention left/behind, so formal spatial relations are essentially absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers good attribute detail for detected items but misses multiple actors and lacks explicit spatial relations, yielding moderate-to-low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of seven pedestrians and two of six vehicles, so many objects and annotations are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attribute labels like age and motion status but misses several communicative and transporting annotations and fails to annotate most objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few relation claims but they are sparse, do not cover most entities, and contradict the ground truth contextual labels such as crossing versus walking along the road.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows some correct atomic attributes but is largely incomplete and contains incorrect contextual and spatial details, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth contains seven pedestrians and four vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It provides some attributes like atomic action, age and door/trunk states but misses many actors and specific contexts such as jaywalking, carrying and talking on phone, reducing its semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reported but they are sparse, partially inaccurate (claims a pedestrian is at a pedestrian crossing while ground truth indicates jaywalking and road-walking) and absent for most objects.
justifications.overall: Given the large number of missing actors, incomplete attributes, and some incorrect context labels, the extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles from the ground truth, so overall object coverage is limited despite partial vehicle detection.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM captures basic attributes like action, transport, and vehicle state for some objects but misses several important labels in the ground truth such as multiple pedestrians, communicative actions, and complex contexts like jaywalking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty and several simple context labels disagree with the ground truth (many ground truth pedestrians are annotated as walking on the road while the extraction only uses side-of-road), so spatial understanding is poor.
justifications.overall: Averaging limited object coverage, partial semantic attribute recovery, and weak spatial relation reporting yields a below‑acceptable overall extraction quality with notable omissions in pedestrians and contextual cues.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 10 relevant objects (2/5 pedestrians, 2/5 vehicles) and missed several persons and vehicles so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes per entity (actions, context, motion, doors) but missed some communicative/context labels and did not annotate all objects, so richness is moderate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations fields are empty despite track hints mentioning relative location, so spatial relation extraction is essentially missing.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a subset of objects and some useful attributes but omits many entities and spatial relations, resulting in limited overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 11 ground truth objects and missed most pedestrians and several vehicles, yielding low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured basic attributes like action, simple context, age, and vehicle states for some objects but missed several contextual labels such as jaywalking and talking in group and omitted many objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations were provided despite track hints, so spatial relation information is effectively missing.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction identifies a few objects with basic attributes but lacks coverage and spatial context, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and appears to match one parked vehicle but misses two moving vehicles, so many ground-truth entities are absent.
justifications.semantic_richness: Several action and context labels are incorrect or incomplete (e.g., walking labeled as standing, jaywalking not identified, age of a senior missed) and vehicle motion states are largely wrong or missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are provided and important contextual relations such as jaywalking or walking along the side of the road are not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small subset of actors with limited and partially incorrect semantic detail and no spatial relations, resulting in low overall utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects a couple of pedestrians and vehicles but misses about half of the persons and most vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies several semantic attributes (actions, simple contexts, vehicle states) but omits or misrepresents important labels like communicative behavior for one person and several contextual distinctions.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction leaves spatial_relations empty and provides no explicit relational information despite ground truth contextual spatial cues.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures some correct high-level properties but is incomplete, misses many objects and spatial relations, and lacks several key semantic labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing most entities and lacking track identifiers.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some basic attributes like walking and adult were correctly assigned for the two detected pedestrians and the vehicle motion was correct, but many contextual and communicative labels and additional vehicle states are missing or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided and several simple context distinctions (e.g., jaywalking, walking along the side) were not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct atomic attributes but misses the majority of entities, contextual information, and spatial relationships, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and missed the moving vehicles from the ground truth while adding an unannotated bicycle, so major omissions exist.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several per-object attributes (actions, simple contexts, age, door/trunk states) but omitted complex contextual labels, object IDs, and some context details, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Returned empty spatial_relations arrays and did not structurally encode relative positions from the ground truth despite informal textual hints, so spatial information is essentially absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some object-level semantics but misses the majority of annotated agents and key motion labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 8 annotated agents (2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles), missing three pedestrians and one moving vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute types (actions, context, age, trunk and doors) showing good richness but it mislabels or omits key context distinctions like jaywalking for several pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations arrays are empty and the model only partially encodes location via simple_context for two pedestrians while missing jaywalking and on-road distinctions, so spatial relation capture is poor.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance with reasonable attribute coverage but significant misses in object recall and spatial/contextual accuracy reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, missing one standing pedestrian and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes like actions, contexts, and vehicle states are provided and largely correct for detected objects but several entities are missing, reducing overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite opportunities to describe positions relative to road or other objects.
justifications.overall: Partial correctness for detected entities but significant missing objects and no spatial relations yield a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing one moving car and two pedestrians and including an extra bicycle, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Many attributes were provided (actions, age, motion, door/trunk states) but important context distinctions like jaywalking were missed and one vehicle type was misidentified.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite relevance for scene understanding.
justifications.overall: Partial detection and attribute reporting but missing critical objects and contextual cues, and lacking spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses one of two people (the bicyclist/sitting person) and omits two vehicles compared to ground truth, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful attributes are present such as pedestrian action and vehicle motion and door/trunk states, but key context labels like jaywalking and one vehicle's stopped status are missing and pedestrian context is incorrect for one individual.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations fields are left empty despite track hints and scene context, providing almost no explicit relational information.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides a few correct attributes but misses multiple actors and important contextual and relational details, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Misses one of two pedestrians (the bicycling/sitting person) and two of four vehicles including the stopped vehicle, so many relevant objects are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracts basic attributes (vehicle trunk/doors, parked status for listed cars, pedestrian age/action for one person) but omits key contextual labels (biking, jaywalking) and attributes for the missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Leaves spatial_relations empty despite using informal hints (left/right) and fails to encode important relational context such as a pedestrian crossing the road.
justifications.overall: Partially correct attribute extraction for listed objects but significant object omissions and missing contextual/spatial relations substantially reduce overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 8 pedestrians and the single vehicle, so most pedestrians in the ground truth are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several per-agent attributes (atomic_action, simple_context, communicative, transporting, age) for detected actors but missed many actors and important contextual labels such as jaywalking and walking on the road.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations fields are empty and the extraction lacks explicit relational statements despite some simple_context spatial cues.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives reasonable attribute detail for a small subset of actors and the vehicle but omits most pedestrians and spatial relations, resulting in limited overall coverage.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and one vehicle but missed the moving vehicle and misclassified one pedestrian's atomic action, so only partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (age, communicative, transporting, trunk/doors) and correct labels for one pedestrian and one vehicle, but a key atomic action is incorrect and a vehicle motion status is missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations entries are provided despite contextual hints in track_hints, so explicit spatial relations are not captured.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate extraction with useful semantic detail but critical errors (missing moving vehicle and one atomic-action mismatch) and no spatial relations, yielding a moderate score.

justifications.completeness: Missing one of two persons (the cyclist) and most vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only a single bicycle and one pedestrian.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (age, atomic action, motion status) but omits the biking context/association and many vehicle entries and attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations extracted and no explicit association linking the cyclist to the bicycle despite ground truth indicating a person biking.
justifications.overall: Captures a subset of entities and basic attributes but misses key objects and associations, limiting its alignment with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian instead of two and omits several vehicles from the ground truth, so key entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes many attribute fields (actions, age, vehicle properties) but contains misclassification of the cyclist and some incorrect or incomplete motion/occupancy labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite spatial context being relevant in the scene.
justifications.overall: Some useful semantic attributes are present, but important entities are missed and the cyclist is misrepresented, leading to poor overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: The model mentions a cyclist in the scene context but fails to enumerate the person as a pedestrian and omits all six vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction gives only a brief scene description and lacks object-level attributes such as the person's action, age, and the vehicles' motion or door/trunk states.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships between objects are reported beyond a generic street description, so relative positions and relations are absent.
justifications.overall: The output provides a minimal high-level caption but misses critical entity detections and detailed semantics needed for autonomous driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Missed the pedestrian/cyclist and detected only one vehicle out of five in the ground truth, so coverage of scene entities is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple attributes for the single detected vehicle (type, motion, trunk/doors, spatial relation) but omits the person and most vehicles, limiting overall semantic completeness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a specific relation ('in the middle of the road') for the detected vehicle that is likely incorrect or unsupported and offers no spatial relations for other objects, so spatial information is largely inaccurate or missing.
justifications.overall: Overall low quality due to missing the cyclist and most vehicles plus incorrect or unsupported attribute and spatial assertions for the lone detected vehicle.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 7 people and no vehicles, missing many objects and several key attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like age and a communicative cue for one person but omits many atomic/transport states and all vehicle semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relation annotations were provided for any entities.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and incomplete, offering only partial attribute recognition for two pedestrians and failing to detect vehicles or most contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only 3 of the 6 annotated persons and omits all vehicles, so many entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Basic attributes like atomic actions, simple context and carrying are included for some pedestrians but many contextual labels such as jaywalking, looking into phone, pushing, and biking are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided only generic left/right relations without linking to object ids or describing relations to vehicles or scene features, so spatial information is sparse and ungrounded.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is incomplete and misses key entities and contextual labels, offering limited and ungrounded spatial relations which reduces its usefulness for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 7 people were detected and no vehicles were reported, so most entities and many attributes are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some per-person attributes are provided (age, action, simple context) for the two detections but many detailed labels like transporting for multiple people and all vehicle semantics are absent.
justifications.spatial_relations: A couple of simple relative positions are given but side-of-road versus on-road distinctions and spatial relations for the majority of entities are missing or incorrect.
justifications.overall: Low recall and partial attribute coverage yield a weak overall extraction despite correct labels for a couple of pedestrians.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 of 11 ground-truth actors are reported and no vehicles are detected, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several attribute fields but omits or mislabels important details (e.g., many transporting/pushing labels and jaywalking contexts) and lacks vehicle semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some relative positions are provided for the three detected pedestrians (including an officer at the center), but coverage is sparse and not corroborated across the scene.
justifications.overall: The output captures a few correct pedestrian attributes and limited spatial cues but misses most actors, many key semantics, and all vehicles, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 8 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing most annotated actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several attribute fields (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but many attributes are incorrect or incomplete and vehicles are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides no spatial relations and fails to distinguish 'walking on road' versus 'walking along the side', so spatial detail is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few basic attributes for a subset of pedestrians but omits most actors, vehicles, and spatial detail, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains 7 pedestrians and 4 vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides per-person attributes (action, context, age) but misses several transporting labels from ground truth and introduces a 'traffic officer' label that does not map to the annotated tracks.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes simple left/center/right relations which are useful but limited in scope and potentially inaccurate given the many missing persons and absence of vehicle relations.
justifications.overall: The extraction conveys some basic semantic and spatial information for a few people but fails to detect most objects and several important attributes, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 6 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of entities and annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels like walking/pushing and age but omits many action and context labels and mislabels or omits several ground truth attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations fields are populated and only one pedestrian includes a positional simple context, so spatial information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partially correct for a couple of pedestrians but fails to capture most entities, vehicle information, and many contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Misses two of four people and all vehicles, and omits several contextual fields.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some labels like actions and transporting but omits complex contextual attributes and contains incorrect or unspecified atomic actions for at least one person.
justifications.spatial_relations: Does not provide explicit spatial relations and leaves relational lists empty despite the scene containing positional context.
justifications.overall: Overall extraction is incomplete and partially inaccurate, capturing a subset of actors and a few attributes but missing vehicles, many people, and spatial/contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 annotated persons and no vehicles, missing several objects and associated attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many attribute fields per person but omits or contradicts ground-truth labels (e.g. missing 'pushing' transport and incorrect crossing context), reducing semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reported, but they are sparse, unverifiable against the provided ground truth, and do not cover most actors or any vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially informative but incomplete and contains notable semantic errors, making it only moderately useful for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Extraction identifies only one of multiple pedestrians and one vehicle, missing most ground-truth actors and several key actions like pushing and getting on a vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attribute fields (atomic_action, transporting, age, motion_status, trunk/doors) but many attributes are absent or incorrect and applied to only a single detected instance.
justifications.spatial_relations: spatial_relations arrays are empty and the extraction fails to encode positional relations despite informal track hints being present.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction shows some attribute-level detail for a few objects but has poor coverage and misses critical actors and relations, limiting its usefulness for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians out of 6 and no vehicles, missing several people and the parked vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided multiple attributes like actions, context, age and transport but included an incorrect transport label and omitted other transport annotations such as pushing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captured one correct sidewalk relation but missed road and jaywalking relations and added an unverified relation to a traffic officer, so spatial coverage is limited.
justifications.overall: Partially correct on actions and age but misses most entities, mislabels transport, ignores the vehicle, and thus performs poorly overall.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 10 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of annotated actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes for two people but omits many actor types, complex contexts, transporting details, and all vehicle attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Leaves spatial_relations empty and fails to capture positional cues like on-road versus sidewalk or jaywalking that are present in the annotations.
justifications.overall: Captures a couple of pedestrians with minimal labels but misses most actors, vehicles, and important contextual and spatial information, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 7 ground-truth persons and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing multiple pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields (actions, context, age, vehicle properties) but several action labels and contexts are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were extracted (all arrays empty), so relative positions between objects are not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some semantic attributes but misses the majority of objects and spatial relationships, limiting its utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 6 ground-truth persons and 1 of 6 ground-truth vehicles, so it captures roughly a quarter of the annotated objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: For detected objects the VLM provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, context, age, trunk/door state) but some labels are incorrect or imprecise and many objects are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations arrays are empty despite hints in track descriptions and the scene, so explicit relational information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to substantial missed detections and missing spatial relations, partially offset by reasonably rich attribute labeling for the few detected objects.

justifications.completeness: Many persons and vehicles from the ground truth are missing or not matched, with only two pedestrians and a few vehicles reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple semantic fields such as actions, context, age and transport, but several labels are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reported but they are sparse, coarse, and not consistently aligned with the annotated object set.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic detail is present but low detection completeness and multiple labeling errors substantially reduce overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing multiple actors and vehicle instances.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted several semantic attributes (actions, context, age, transporting) but missed or misassigned many labels (e.g., missing entering-a-building and a senior label) and omitted attributes for some actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relations provided despite ground truth containing positional contexts such as on the road versus along the side of the road, so relational information is lacking.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat accurate attribute extraction for the detected actors but overall misses many actors and relational information, resulting in low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 6 pedestrians and 1 of 6 vehicles, missing most annotated actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes are provided for detected actors (age, actions, motion) but several actors are missing and one bicyclist has an incorrect atomic_action, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations were reported and relative positioning between actors is not captured despite a general location label.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few correct attributes for detected objects but misses the majority of actors and spatial relations, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected the parked vehicle and two pedestrians but missed one walking person and failed to identify one person's age (senior), so not fully complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, contexts, age, vehicle states) but includes an incorrect age and an unsupported simple_context, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations were provided despite spatial cues in simple_contexts, leaving spatial relation information missing.
justifications.overall: Partially complete and semantically informative but contains omissions and an age/context error and lacks explicit spatial relations, yielding a moderate score.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and omitted a pedestrian carrying an object and a senior, though the vehicle was correctly detected.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields but misses important labels (e.g., carrying) and mislabels age for a senior, reducing semantic correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Simple_context entries provide some spatial information but the spatial_relations arrays are empty and actors are not linked to the parked vehicle.
justifications.overall: Partially correct detection with accurate vehicle status but multiple missing pedestrians and attribute errors limit overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Very low completeness: the extraction reports 2 pedestrians while the ground truth contains 6 persons, missing multiple actors and at least one transporting annotation.
justifications.semantic_richness: Low semantic richness: the extraction captures basic actions and age but omits important attributes (e.g., carrying with both hands, talking on phone) and misrepresents some simple contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: High score for spatial relations because both extraction and ground truth provide no explicit spatial relations, so the extraction is consistent with the ground truth in this regard.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to many missed persons and missing critical semantic attributes, despite correctly not inventing spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 8 ground-truth pedestrians and the single vehicle, so the extraction misses the majority of persons in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, transporting, age, communicative) but mislabels a senior as 'adult', omits several agents, and has some incorrect transport annotations, giving moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Lists several spatial relations, but the ground truth contains no spatial annotations to verify them and some relations appear speculative, so accuracy and coverage are limited.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures useful details for a few agents (vehicle and three pedestrians) but misses many pedestrians and contains attribute errors, leading to below-average overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth persons and missed several actors (including a standing person and others), so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the two detected pedestrians the extraction provides multiple attributes (action, context, transporting, age), but it omits three other persons and some contextual details, so richness is moderate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations fields are left empty despite track hints indicating left/right positions, so explicit spatial relation annotations are effectively missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives useful attribute detail for detected individuals but misses the majority of persons and lacks explicit spatial relations, yielding below-average overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians out of 6 annotated, missing four persons including one carrying an object and one on the phone.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided basic labels like atomic actions and age but missed key attributes such as transporting and communicative behaviors present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relations were provided and some simple contexts conflict with ground truth (for example road versus sidewalk), so spatial information is largely missing.
justifications.overall: Low recall of pedestrians and missing important attributes lead to a low overall quality despite correctly labeling some atomic actions.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing several objects and attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic atomic actions for two individuals but omits or mislabels communicative and transporting attributes and lacks coverage of other persons.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite multiple pedestrians and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Extraction is incomplete and shallow, identifying a couple of actors correctly but missing many objects, relations, and important attributes.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 of the 6 ground-truth pedestrians were reported and no vehicles were detected, so many actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (atomic actions, transporting) are provided but several key labels (communicative behaviors, complex contexts) and many person-level distinctions are missing or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations fields are empty and the extraction fails to capture positional relations present in the scene (e.g., near shop, entering building).
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct person-level attributes but misses half the pedestrians, all vehicles, and many contextual and spatial details, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: 'Detected only one of two annotated persons and omitted many attributes, so coverage is low.'
justifications.semantic_richness: 'Provided some attribute fields but many values contradict ground truth (communicative and transporting) and a second person is missing.'
justifications.spatial_relations: 'No spatial_relations provided despite positional cues and one person missing, so score is zero.'
justifications.overall: 'Missed one person and many semantic attributes, with incorrect context labels, resulting in poor overall extraction.'

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 ground-truth persons and 1 of 2 vehicles and missed several actors and attributes, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some semantic attributes (actions, communicative, age) but omits important labels like 'carrying with both hands' and 'pushing' and uses many 'none of the above' placeholders.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations arrays are empty despite scene hints (e.g., 'standing near boxes' in the track hint), so relational information is not captured.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a subset of simple attributes correctly but misses multiple entities, relations, and finer-grained contextual labels, limiting its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses one of the two pedestrians, fails to capture the transporting action for the standing person, and misidentifies the vehicle, so many ground-truth entities and attributes are absent or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: A few correct attributes are present (standing, adult) but key semantics are missing or wrong (pushing omitted, walking pedestrian missing, vehicle type and motion incorrect), yielding limited richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The ground truth contains no explicit spatial relations so the empty relations are not heavily penalized, but the extraction also fails to provide object-to-object spatial links despite having track hints, so only a moderate score is merited.
justifications.overall: Several missed entities and incorrect labels combined with omitted important actions result in low overall quality despite a couple of correct attributes.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses the single pedestrian and the majority of vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only two vehicles and no pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: It provides some attributes (motion_status, trunk_open, doors_open, spatial_relations) but mislabels vehicle type and omits pedestrian attributes and many vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a couple of vague relations like 'next to the building' and 'next to the sidewalk' which are sparse and incomplete for the scene's objects.
justifications.overall: Low completeness and only partial, sometimes inaccurate semantic details produce an overall weak extraction despite correct parking statuses for the listed vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Missed all pedestrians (2) and detected only two of three vehicles, so many ground-truth entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several vehicle attributes but mislabels a van as a truck, assigns closed attributes where GT is unknown, and omits all pedestrian semantics, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite scene context that could support them.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some vehicle attributes correctly but misses pedestrians entirely, contains a vehicle type error, and provides no spatial relations, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports 3 vehicles and no pedestrians while the ground truth contains 7 vehicles and 1 person, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides object types and door/trunk/motion attributes for reported vehicles but omits track identifiers, misses the person and related human actions, and lacks attributes for several ground-truth vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are given (next to the building, inside the garage) which are useful, but they are coarse and not validated against precise ground-truth locations.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful attribute-level details for a subset of vehicles and rough spatial cues but fails to detect many objects including the pedestrian, resulting in a below-average overall result.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two ground-truth pedestrians and one of two vehicles, missing the person who is bending/closing and the second vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected instances the extraction provides several semantic attributes (action, context, age, vehicle door/trunk states) but it omits the second person's action and other object instances, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations are provided (empty arrays), so inter-object and object-to-scene relationships are entirely missing.
justifications.overall: Partial correctness for the reported objects with accurate attributes for one pedestrian and one vehicle, but missing the second pedestrian and vehicle and lacking spatial relationships leads to a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Captured the pedestrian attributes but missed the annotated parked vehicle and did not include object track IDs, so it is only partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several pedestrian attributes (action, age, context) but omitted vehicle motion status and track identifiers, reducing overall semantic richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Failed to populate spatial_relations despite ground truth indicating the person is along the side of the road, so spatial relations are largely missing.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate for the pedestrian but missing vehicle information and spatial relations, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected the parked vehicle and one of two pedestrians but missed the second person who is bending and closing, so partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes for the detected pedestrian and vehicle but misclassifies the vehicle type and omits the second person's actions, reducing semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations were provided despite track hints mentioning left/right, and the ground truth contains no spatial relations, so the extraction is neither particularly strong nor harmful.
justifications.overall: Partially complete and semantically mixed due to a missed person and a vehicle type error, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Both ground-truth persons are detected and annotated, though an extra vehicle false positive was reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (actions, age, contexts) but mislabels one person context and invents a vehicle not in the ground truth, reducing semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are reasonable (near building, side of road) but others are imprecise or unsupported and include relations for a false positive vehicle.
justifications.overall: Detection recall for people is good but semantic errors and a false positive vehicle lower overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains around 36 persons and 2 vehicles, so object coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple per-person attributes (atomic_action, simple_context, communicative, transporting, age) and vehicle states, offering moderate semantic detail despite some inaccuracies and omissions.
justifications.spatial_relations: All spatial_relations arrays are empty, so the extraction provides no information about relative positions or object-to-object relations in the scene.
justifications.overall: Overall quality is low due to very limited object detection and missing spatial relations, partially offset by reasonably rich per-object attributes for the few detected entities.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth lists about 30 pedestrians and a vehicle, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple attribute types (atomic action, context, age, vehicle properties) for the few detected entities but omits many communicative/contextual labels present in the ground truth and misstates the vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but they are sparse, relative-only, and include a likely error about the vehicle being stopped/in the crosswalk contrary to the ground truth moving vehicle.
justifications.overall: The output gives useful attribute detail for a small subset of actors but is severely incomplete and contains at least one incorrect vehicle state, reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only three pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains many more pedestrians (~28) and two vehicles, so a large portion of objects is missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies basic labels (atomic action, simple context, age) for a few detections but omits many semantic details present in the ground truth (object identifiers, child/senior labels for several people, communicative annotations for grouped talkers, and correct vehicle motion states).
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations are provided despite the scene containing clear locational contexts (crossing vs along the side) and likely inter-object relations, so this aspect is effectively missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small subset of scene entities with some basic semantics but misses most objects, many important attributes, and any spatial relation information, yielding poor overall coverage and detail.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 of about 25 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles were reported, so the extraction misses the large majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: While several attribute fields are present, many are wrong or oversimplified (all pedestrians marked adult, communicative cues and varied contexts often omitted).
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations arrays are empty and the extraction fails to encode positional relations like crossing at the pedestrian crossing or group formations.
justifications.overall: Overall score is low due to very poor recall of pedestrians and several incorrect or missing attributes despite correctly identifying one moving truck and its door/trunk state.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists about 30 people and one vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields (atomic action, context, age) but entries are generic or incorrect for many actors and it misses communicative and transporting labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are provided for any detected pedestrian despite clear grouping and crossing distinctions in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures only a tiny subset of entities and supplies limited and partly incorrect semantic details, resulting in low overall utility for perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction found only 4 pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth lists over 20 persons, missing the majority of entities and at least one different age label.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provided several attribute fields (action, simple context, age) but misassigned many contexts as crossing, omitted multiple communicative labels, and failed to capture the one senior label, reducing semantic alignment with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported for any pedestrian or vehicle despite clear spatial context (crosswalk, side of road) present in the scene.
justifications.overall: While basic actions and vehicle status were often correct, the extraction has very low recall, several incorrect or missing contextual and communicative attributes, and no spatial relations, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth includes many more persons and one parked vehicle, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple attribute fields per person but misses or mislabels key attributes (e.g., child vs adult, falsely reports carrying) and omits most individuals, so the semantic detail is limited and partially incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite group/communicative relationships in the ground truth, so relational context is essentially absent.
justifications.overall: Sparse and partially incorrect extraction that captures a few actors with some attributes but fails to detect most pedestrians, the parked vehicle, and important contextual/relational labels.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains over 25 persons and a vehicle, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic actions and age for 3 people but misses many important context labels such as jaywalking, talking, carrying, and any vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite multiple pedestrians having road versus sidewalk contexts in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few simple attributes for a small subset of people but is largely incomplete and lacks richer semantic and spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains 40+ persons and multiple vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the few detected people the model reports basic attributes (age, atomic action, simple context) correctly but it omits many specific behavioral labels present in the ground truth such as jaywalking, carrying/pulling, and various communicative states.
justifications.spatial_relations: All spatial_relations are empty despite ground truth distinctions like walking on the road vs sidewalk and multiple jaywalking instances, so spatial relationships are effectively not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparsely populated and underdetects most actors and spatial behaviors, offering limited semantic value for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 24 persons and 6 vehicles in the ground truth, so the extraction misses the vast majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful attributes (atomic_action, simple_context, age) for the few detected actors but omits many per-object attributes present in the ground truth and provides no detail for most entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty for all entities despite contextual hints and multiple nearby actors/vehicles, so relational information is effectively absent.
justifications.overall: While the extracted labels for the few detected objects are broadly reasonable, the system severely under-detects objects and lacks relational and comprehensive semantic coverage, resulting in low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Only two pedestrians were reported out of many annotated persons and the single vehicle was omitted, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model captured basic atomic actions and a simple scene description for the two detected people but missed many contextual labels (jaywalking, communicative cues, transporting) and vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: No structured spatial_relations were provided (arrays empty) and only informal left/right hints appear in track hints, so spatial information is minimal and unstructured.
justifications.overall: Significant omissions in object detection and contextual labeling result in a poor overall extraction despite correct simple action labels for two pedestrians.

justifications.completeness: Detected only four pedestrians and no vehicle while the ground truth contains many more persons and one vehicle, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction reports basic attributes (atomic_action, age, simple_context) for a few people but misses many important labels present in ground truth such as jaywalking, carrying, communicative cues, and vehicle state.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty and no explicit inter-object spatial relationships were provided despite spatial context differences in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and misses most entities and key semantic labels from the ground truth, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth lists many more persons and four vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels like walking and age but misses many communicative and transporting attributes present in ground truth, yielding limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite multiple actors and road/sidewalk contexts, so this is missing entirely.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few basic pedestrian attributes but fails to cover most entities, detailed attributes, and spatial relations, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 4 pedestrians and 1 vehicle compared to over 20 pedestrians and 2 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects and several transporting annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides basic attribute fields but omits many important labels present in the ground truth such as multiple transporting cases, communicative behaviors, age variation, and complex contextual tags.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations arrays are empty and several simple_context labels are overly uniform or incorrect relative to ground truth distinctions like on-road versus sidewalk and jaywalking.
justifications.overall: The output captures a few basic attributes for a small subset of actors but fails to detect most entities and many critical contextual and spatial details, resulting in low overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: Detected 6 of 19 annotated objects (3 of 15 pedestrians and 3 of 4 vehicles) and missed many pedestrians, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides structured attributes (actions, contexts, motion) but misses many context variants and contains incorrect transporting and context labels, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite opportunities to describe relative positions, so this is missing.
justifications.overall: Low coverage and missing spatial relations, combined with several incorrect contextual labels, yield a poor overall extraction.

justifications.completeness: Only three pedestrians are reported versus 18 annotated and several pedestrian attributes and contexts are entirely missing, though vehicle entries roughly match the number of ground truth vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute types (atomic action, simple context, communicative, transporting, age) and vehicle states, but it omits many annotated communicative/contextual labels and includes at least one incorrect transporting claim.
justifications.spatial_relations: Local relations around the truck are captured for the reported actors, but broader and more specific spatial/contextual relations from the ground truth such as crossing or walking along the side are missing and most actors lack any spatial relation.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows reasonable semantic variety and some correct vehicle motion labels but is severely incomplete for pedestrians and contains some incorrect attribute assignments, limiting its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 5 of 20 ground-truth objects (2/16 pedestrians, 3/4 vehicles), indicating low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted several attribute types (actions, context, age, motion) but missed or incorrectly labeled many attributes (e.g., claiming a person is 'carrying with both hands' when GT indicates none, missing the child) and overlooked many pedestrians, so semantic accuracy is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Left all spatial_relations empty despite many pedestrians having spatial/simple-context annotations like crossing or jaywalking, so spatial relations are not captured.
justifications.overall: Low completeness combined with attribute errors (incorrect transporting, missing subjects) and absent spatial relations results in poor overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 3 of ~26 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles from the ground truth, so overall detection coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many semantic attributes (actions, context, transporting, age) but several labels are incorrect or missing compared to ground truth such as standing, sitting, talking in group and child ages.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present and potentially useful but are vague, not tied to explicit track IDs, and cannot be fully validated against the ground truth annotations.
justifications.overall: While semantically rich in format, the extraction suffers from poor coverage and some incorrect labels, yielding a low overall quality for this frame.

justifications.completeness: Vehicles are correctly reported but the extraction lists only three pedestrians while the ground truth contains many more, so overall entity coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies basic attributes (atomic action, simple context, age) for detected actors but misses many important labels (numerous pedestrian instances, child ages, communicative states like 'talking in group', and crossing-specific contexts).
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite ground-truth contexts indicating crossing, walking along the side, and other positional relations.
justifications.overall: Accurate on a small subset (vehicles and a few pedestrians) but major omissions in pedestrian count, contextual detail, and spatial relations limit the extraction's usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three pedestrians and three vehicles while ground truth contains many more pedestrian instances and not all vehicles are clearly matched.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted useful attributes per entity (atomic action, simple context, age, vehicle door/trunk status) but missed several ground truth contextual labels and many entities entirely.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides coarse left/right/front relations that roughly align with scene layout and vehicle motion but lacks precise correspondences to the many ground truth objects.
justifications.overall: Low entity coverage severely limits overall utility despite reasonably rich attributes for the few detected objects and coarse spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only one pedestrian and omits a second person and the vehicle present in the ground truth, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like atomic action and a coarse age estimate are given, but age is misclassified and several context labels and the second person attributes are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite multiple actors and a vehicle being present, so relational information is essentially absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and lacks critical objects, correct attribute values, and relational context, yielding a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only lists 3 pedestrians while the ground truth contains 40+ persons and it captures 2 of 3 vehicles, so overall object coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many per-object attributes (actions, context, transporting, age, etc.) beyond the ground truth but includes incorrect assertions (e.g., transporting carried with both hands) and misses many individuals.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some pairwise relations are supplied linking pedestrians and vehicles, but they are sparse, not tied to ground-truth IDs, and contain potential inconsistencies, making their reliability moderate at best.
justifications.overall: While semantically rich for the few detected objects, the extraction's very low recall of pedestrians and some incorrect attribute labels reduce its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Missed the vehicle completely and misidentified key pedestrian attributes such as age and one pedestrian's action, so many ground-truth elements are absent or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a variety of attributes and scene context but several critical attributes are wrong (ages, actions) and the vehicle annotation is missing, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible spatial relations (on the road, next to the wall) that appear accurate for the pedestrians, making spatial information relatively reliable despite other errors.
justifications.overall: Overall quality is limited by the missing vehicle and multiple attribute misclassifications, with some strengths in attribute coverage and spatial cues but insufficient accuracy for reliable use.

justifications.completeness: Misses the annotated vehicle and mislabels atomic actions and ages for both people, so several key ground truth attributes are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides scene context and multiple pedestrian attributes but omits the vehicle, uses generic age labels instead of specific senior category, and fails to assign the correct atomic action for the cyclist.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captures plausible spatial relations for both pedestrians (on the road and next to the wall) that align with the scene description.
justifications.overall: Contains useful descriptive details but has significant errors and omissions (missing vehicle, incorrect ages and at least one atomic action), resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extractor captured a cyclist and motion but completely omitted the person as a pedestrian and missed person attributes such as age and action.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful scene context and motion were provided but key semantic details are missing or misattributed, for example the cyclist was recorded under vehicles and trunk and door states were inappropriately reported.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relations were returned despite a track hint about the bicycle being on the right and the scene description mentioning a tunnel, so relational information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to misclassification of the cyclist as a vehicle, omission of the pedestrian record and attributes, and missing relational details despite some correct motion and scene cues.

justifications.completeness: The extraction includes the single person and correctly labels the atomic action and other context fields, though it mislabels age compared to ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides basic semantic labels and a descriptive scene phrase but lacks the age specificity and additional contextual detail present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The scene description mentions sitting against a wall and a right-side location but the structured spatial_relations field is empty, missing explicit relations.
justifications.overall: Detection and action labeling are correct and complete, but the incorrect age classification and absence of structured spatial relations reduce overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Detected the single person and correct atomic action and absence of vehicles but misclassified age and added incorrect simple context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes beyond the ground truth such as clothing and scene description but includes incorrect age and context, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Did not provide explicit spatial relation annotations and its implied location contradicts ground truth simple context, so spatial detail is lacking.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate detection and action labeling but incorrect age and erroneous contextual labeling limit its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected the person and correctly labeled the atomic action and communicative/transporting fields but missed or misclassified important attributes such as age and simple context.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many semantic fields (scene description, action, context, age, communicative, transporting) but some key entries are incorrect which reduces its utility.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were present in either the ground truth or the extraction, so the extraction is correct for this category.
justifications.overall: Overall the person is detected and several attributes are provided, but the incorrect age and mismatched simple context notably degrade the extraction quality.
